status_message: Ash, your recovery is holding steady at 54% — solid Yellow status for training today! Your HRV is consistent at 35.6ms, showing your nervous system is stable. With 77% sleep performance, you're doing well despite unknown sleep hours. Time for a MODERATE intensity workout at the office gym today — push hard but don't overextend. You're building momentum on Day 6!
weekly_insight: This week shows recovery stabilization after a slight dip on Monday-Wednesday. Your HRV remains consistently around 35-36ms, which is normal for a beginner; as fitness improves, this will increase. Sleep performance is steady at 77%, but logging actual sleep hours will help optimize recovery — aim for 7-8 hours nightly to boost that Yellow to Green.
monthly_insight: With only 1 workout tracked across 30 days, your program is still ramping up, which is appropriate for a beginner. Recovery averaging 52.7% suggests you're managing stress well, but expect this to improve as your body adapts to consistent training. Focus on hitting all 5-6 gym sessions per week for the next 2 weeks to accelerate fat loss and core definition.

workout.rationale: 54% recovery = Yellow zone. Safe for moderate intensity training without overextending. Tuesday office gym session — focus on building upper body strength while engaging core for six-pack visibility. Avoid heavy lower body to preserve recovery for Friday's session.
workout.warmup: 5 minutes: 2 min light jogging or brisk walking on treadmill, 30 sec arm circles (10 each direction), 30 sec bodyweight squats (15 reps slow), 1 min arm swings and shoulder rolls. Finish with 2 min dynamic stretching (leg swings, torso twists).
workout.cooldown: 5 minutes: 2 min slow walking to lower heart rate, then: Chest stretch (30 sec each side), Shoulder stretch (30 sec each side), Seated forward fold (30 sec), Child's pose (30 sec). Breathe deeply — 4 counts in, 6 counts out.

exercises.name: Push-ups (or Incline Push-ups on bench)
exercises.notes: Keep elbows at 45° angle, not flaring out. Lower chest to 2 inches above ground. Core tight — imagine bracing for a punch. If full push-ups too hard, do incline push-ups on gym bench or wall push-ups.
exercises.youtube_search: proper push-up form beginner full range of motion

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rows (single arm, each side)
exercises.notes: Hinge at hips, one knee on bench. Pull dumbbell to ribcage, squeeze shoulder blade. Don't rotate torso. Use light weight (5-8kg) to master form — quality over ego.
exercises.youtube_search: single arm dumbbell row proper form beginner

exercises.name: Plank Hold (bodyweight)
exercises.notes: Shoulders over wrists, body in straight line from head to heels. Engage core — don't let hips sag or pike up. This directly strengthens your six-pack. Breathe steadily.
exercises.youtube_search: perfect plank form beginner core engagement

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press (seated or standing)
exercises.notes: Dumbbells at shoulder height, elbows 45° forward. Press straight up, full lockout at top. Control on way down — 2 seconds down. Avoid arching lower back excessively.
exercises.youtube_search: dumbbell shoulder press beginner form seated

exercises.name: Dead Bug (core work, bodyweight)
exercises.notes: Lie on back, arms extended up, knees at 90°. Lower opposite arm and leg simultaneously, tap floor, return. Move slowly and controlled. This teaches core stability for six-pack definition.
exercises.youtube_search: dead bug exercise proper form beginner core

meals.name: Breakfast
meals.description: Overnight Oats with Strawberry & Almond: Mix 50g rolled oats + 250ml whole milk + 1 tbsp honey in container, refrigerate overnight. Top with 100g fresh strawberries (sliced), 15g sliced almonds, pinch of cinnamon. Creamy, satisfying, protein-rich base. WITH BREAKFAST: Take Multivitamin, Vitamin D3 (2000 IU), Omega-3 (1000mg), Vitamin E (400 IU), Zinc (15mg) with water.

meals.name: Mid-Morning Snack
meals.description: 125g Activia yogurt (plain or vanilla) + 10 whole almonds. The Activia yogurt is your daily probiotic anchor — essential for gut health and digestion during fat loss.

meals.name: Lunch
meals.description: Moong Dal Rice Bowl: 1.5 cups cooked white rice (200g) + 1 cup cooked moong dal (200g) mixed with 1/2 tbsp ghee, salt, cumin seeds. Side: shredded cucumber + carrot salad (raw, 100g). This is a classic Indian protein powerhouse — moong dal absorbs easily and is perfect for fat loss.

meals.name: Pre-Workout Snack
meals.description: 1 medium banana (120g) + 10g peanut butter mixed in. Quick-digesting carbs for energy. TAKE: Pre-workout supplement (follow packet dosing) + Fiber capsule (1g) 30 minutes before heading to office gym at 5:30pm.

meals.name: Post-Workout Shake
meals.description: 1 scoop protein powder (vanilla or chocolate flavored) + 300ml whole milk stirred well. Drink immediately within 30 minutes of finishing workout. This locks in muscle protein synthesis for recovery.

meals.name: Dinner
meals.description: Egg Bhurji with Roti: 3 large eggs scrambled with 1/2 onion (diced), 1/2 tomato (diced), 1 green chili (minced), turmeric, salt — cooked in 1 tsp oil. Serve with 2 medium rotis (whole wheat). High protein, satisfying, traditional Indian comfort meal. TAKE: Magnesium supplement (400mg) 30 minutes before bed to improve sleep quality and recovery.

daily_tips: Yellow recovery (54%) is ideal for Tuesday moderate-intensity work — you're strong enough to push but wise enough not to exceed capacity. This builds sustainable progress without burnout.

Your moong dal lunch is 'liquid gold' for six-pack seekers: high protein, low fat, easy digestion, and a complete amino acid profile. Dal + rice combinations are Ayurvedic perfection for body recomposition.

Your sleep performance is 77% despite unknown hours logged — tomorrow morning, start tracking actual bedtime and wake time in your WHOOP app. Even 1 extra hour of quality sleep will push recovery from Yellow to Green and accelerate fat loss.
progress_insight: Ash, after 6 days and only 1 tracked workout, you're at a critical momentum point. Your recovery is stable (54%), HRV is consistent (35.6ms), and your sleep performance is solid (77%) — this foundation is excellent. However, the low strain (4.1/21) indicates you haven't yet challenged your body hard enough to see visible six-pack definition. This week, commit to ALL 5 gym sessions (Mon, Tue, Wed, Fri + one weekend walk) and hit the 1800 kcal target daily with 130g protein. Your moong dal, egg, and paneer rotations are perfect; consistency over the next 3 weeks will show visible abs. Focus: Don't miss a single session.
